concepts.title: Causes of Partition
concepts.description: The partition of India was primarily caused by the dispute between the Indian National Congress and the Muslim League over the issue of Pakistan. The Congress wanted a unified India, while the Muslim League demanded a separate homeland for Muslims.

concepts.title: Radcliffe Line
concepts.description: The Radcliffe Line was a boundary demarcating the border between India and Pakistan, drawn by Sir Cyril Radcliffe. It was a hastily drawn line that led to significant territorial disputes and migrations between India and Pakistan.

concepts.title: Role of British in Partition
concepts.description: The British played a significant role in the partition of India by creating and fueling the communal tensions between Hindus and Muslims. They used the divide-and-rule policy to maintain their control over India and eventually left the country to the warring factions.

concepts.title: Key Events of Partition
concepts.description: To remember the key events of the partition, use the mnemonic 'BORDER': B - British Leave, O - Outbreak of Violence, R - Refugees, D - Division of India, E - Evacuation, R - Rioting, and T - Transfer of Power.

concepts.title: Misconception about Gandhi's Role
concepts.description: One common mistake is to assume that Mahatma Gandhi was opposed to the partition of India. However, Gandhi initially supported the idea of a separate homeland for Muslims, but later changed his stance and advocated for a unified India.

concepts.title: Timeline of Events Leading to Partition
concepts.description: Key events leading to partition: (i) 1906 - Aligarh Movement, (ii) 1916 - Lucknow Pact, (iii) 1920 - Non-Cooperation Movement, (iv) 1930 - Round Table Conferences, (v) 1946 - Direct Action Day, and (vi) 1947 - Independence and Partition.

concepts.title: Number of Refugees
concepts.description: Approximately 14.5 million people were displaced during the partition, with around 1.5 million killed in the communal violence.

concepts.title: Role of Jinnah in Partition
concepts.description: Muhammad Ali Jinnah, the leader of the Muslim League, played a crucial role in the demand for a separate homeland for Muslims, which ultimately led to the partition of India.
